prevalence.value: common; high burden in early childhood
prevalence.source: Pediatric infection epidemiology syntheses / WHO context

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; contributes to hearing loss risk when recurrent/untreated

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with otoscopy; signs of effusion and inflammation; differentiate from OME

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; delayed care can increase chronic ear disease risk

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Otoscopy quality and access vary; many cases treated empirically
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Analgesia; antibiotics (amoxicillin) for selected cases; referral for recurrent disease and hearing concerns
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Overuse of antibiotics and limited ENT services in many districts
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Availability of low-cost antibiotics and expanding child health services
